solution:
```
class TextEditor:

    def __init__(self):
        self.text = ''
        self.cursor = 0

    def addText(self, text: str) -> None:
        self.text += text
        self.cursor += len(text)

    def deleteText(self, k: int) -> int:
        deleted = min(k, self.cursor)
        self.text = self.text[deleted:]
        self.cursor -= deleted
        return deleted

    def cursorLeft(self, k: int) -> str:
        self.cursor = max(0, self.cursor - k)
        return self.text[self.cursor - min(10, self.cursor):self.cursor]

    def cursorRight(self, k: int) -> str:
        self.cursor += k
        return self.text[max(0, self.cursor - 10):self.cursor]
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1) for addText, deleteText, cursorLeft, cursorRight
space complexity: O(n) for storing the text
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a simple string and integer to keep track of the text and cursor position. The addText method appends the new text to the end of the current text and updates the cursor position. The deleteText method deletes the specified number of characters from the left of the cursor, updating the cursor position and text accordingly. The cursorLeft and cursorRight methods update the cursor position and return the desired substring. The time complexity is O(1) for all methods, making it efficient for large inputs.
